version: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164, Subpart C
note: US Government work — public domain. R = Required, A = Addressable per 45 CFR 164.306(d).

controls.title: Security Management Process — Standard
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to prevent, detect, contain, and correct security violations.

controls.title: Risk Analysis
controls.description: Conduct an accurate and thorough assessment of the potential risks and vulnerabilities to the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of ePHI.

controls.title: Risk Management
controls.description: Implement security measures sufficient to reduce risks and vulnerabilities to a reasonable and appropriate level.

controls.title: Sanction Policy
controls.description: Apply appropriate sanctions against workforce members who fail to comply with security policies and procedures.

controls.title: Information System Activity Review
controls.description: Implement procedures to regularly review records of information system activity, such as audit logs, access reports, and security incident tracking reports.

controls.title: Assigned Security Responsibility — Standard
controls.description: Identify the security official who is responsible for the development and implementation of security policies and procedures.

controls.title: Workforce Security — Standard
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to ensure that all members of the workforce have appropriate access to ePHI, and to prevent unauthorized access.

controls.title: Authorization and/or Supervision
controls.description: Implement procedures for the authorization and/or supervision of workforce members who work with ePHI or in locations where it might be accessed.

controls.title: Workforce Clearance Procedure
controls.description: Implement procedures to determine that the access of a workforce member to ePHI is appropriate.

controls.title: Termination Procedures
controls.description: Implement procedures for terminating access to ePHI when the employment of, or other arrangement with, a workforce member ends.

controls.title: Information Access Management — Standard
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures for authorizing access to ePHI that are consistent with the applicable requirements of subpart E of this part.

controls.title: Isolating Health Care Clearinghouse Functions
controls.description: If a health care clearinghouse is part of a larger organization, the clearinghouse must implement policies and procedures that protect the ePHI of the clearinghouse from unauthorized access by the larger organization.

controls.title: Access Authorization
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures for granting access to ePHI, for example, through access to a workstation, transaction, program, process, or other mechanism.

controls.title: Access Establishment and Modification
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures that, based upon the covered entity's access authorization policies, establish, document, review, and modify a user's right of access to a workstation, transaction, program, or process.

controls.title: Security Awareness and Training — Standard
controls.description: Implement a security awareness and training program for all members of the workforce (including management).

controls.title: Security Reminders
controls.description: Periodic security updates.

controls.title: Protection from Malicious Software
controls.description: Procedures for guarding against, detecting, and reporting malicious software.

controls.title: Log-In Monitoring
controls.description: Procedures for monitoring log-in attempts and reporting discrepancies.

controls.title: Password Management
controls.description: Procedures for creating, changing, and safeguarding passwords.

controls.title: Security Incident Procedures — Standard
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to address security incidents.

controls.title: Response and Reporting
controls.description: Identify and respond to suspected or known security incidents; mitigate, to the extent practicable, harmful effects of security incidents that are known; and document security incidents and their outcomes.

controls.title: Contingency Plan — Standard
controls.description: Establish (and implement as needed) policies and procedures for responding to an emergency or other occurrence that damages systems that contain ePHI.

controls.title: Data Backup Plan
controls.description: Establish and implement procedures to create and maintain retrievable exact copies of ePHI.

controls.title: Disaster Recovery Plan
controls.description: Establish (and implement as needed) procedures to restore any loss of data.

controls.title: Emergency Mode Operation Plan
controls.description: Establish (and implement as needed) procedures to enable continuation of critical business processes for protection of the security of ePHI while operating in emergency mode.

controls.title: Testing and Revision Procedures
controls.description: Implement procedures for periodic testing and revision of contingency plans.

controls.title: Applications and Data Criticality Analysis
controls.description: Assess the relative criticality of specific applications and data in support of other contingency plan components.

controls.title: Evaluation — Standard
controls.description: Perform a periodic technical and nontechnical evaluation, based initially upon the standards implemented under this rule, that establishes the extent to which a covered entity's security policies and procedures meet the requirements.

controls.title: Business Associate Contracts and Other Arrangements — Standard
controls.description: A covered entity may permit a business associate to create, receive, maintain, or transmit ePHI on its behalf only if the covered entity obtains satisfactory assurances that the business associate will appropriately safeguard the information.

controls.title: Facility Access Controls — Standard
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to limit physical access to its electronic information systems and the facility or facilities in which they are housed, while ensuring that properly authorized access is allowed.

controls.title: Contingency Operations
controls.description: Establish (and implement as needed) procedures that allow facility access in support of restoration of lost data under the disaster recovery plan and emergency mode operations plan in the event of an emergency.

controls.title: Facility Security Plan
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to safeguard the facility and the equipment therein from unauthorized physical access, tampering, and theft.

controls.title: Access Control and Validation Procedures
controls.description: Implement procedures to control and validate a person's access to facilities based on their role or function, including visitor control, and control of access to software programs for testing and revision.

controls.title: Maintenance Records
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to document repairs and modifications to the physical components of a facility which are related to security.

controls.title: Workstation Use — Standard
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures that specify the proper functions to be performed, the manner in which those functions are to be performed, and the physical attributes of the surroundings of a specific workstation or class of workstation that can access ePHI.

controls.title: Workstation Security — Standard
controls.description: Implement physical safeguards for all workstations that access ePHI, to restrict access to authorized users.

controls.title: Device and Media Controls — Standard
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures that govern the receipt and removal of hardware and electronic media that contain ePHI into and out of a facility, and the movement of these items within the facility.

controls.title: Disposal
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to address the final disposition of ePHI, and/or the hardware or electronic media on which it is stored.

controls.title: Media Re-Use
controls.description: Implement procedures for removal of ePHI from electronic media before the media are made available for re-use.

controls.title: Accountability (media tracking)
controls.description: Maintain a record of the movements of hardware and electronic media and any person responsible therefore.

controls.title: Data Backup and Storage
controls.description: Create a retrievable, exact copy of ePHI, when needed, before movement of equipment.

controls.title: Access Control — Standard
controls.description: Implement technical policies and procedures for electronic information systems that maintain ePHI to allow access only to those persons or software programs that have been granted access rights.

controls.title: Unique User Identification
controls.description: Assign a unique name and/or number for identifying and tracking user identity.

controls.title: Emergency Access Procedure
controls.description: Establish (and implement as needed) procedures for obtaining necessary ePHI during an emergency.

controls.title: Automatic Logoff
controls.description: Implement electronic procedures that terminate an electronic session after a predetermined time of inactivity.

controls.title: Encryption and Decryption (at rest)
controls.description: Implement a mechanism to encrypt and decrypt ePHI.

controls.title: Audit Controls — Standard
controls.description: Implement hardware, software, and/or procedural mechanisms that record and examine activity in information systems that contain or use ePHI.

controls.title: Integrity — Standard
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to protect ePHI from improper alteration or destruction.

controls.title: Mechanism to Authenticate ePHI
controls.description: Implement electronic mechanisms to corroborate that ePHI has not been altered or destroyed in an unauthorized manner.

controls.title: Person or Entity Authentication — Standard
controls.description: Implement procedures to verify that a person or entity seeking access to ePHI is the one claimed.

controls.title: Transmission Security — Standard
controls.description: Implement technical security measures to guard against unauthorized access to ePHI that is being transmitted over an electronic communications network.

controls.title: Integrity Controls
controls.description: Implement security measures to ensure that electronically transmitted ePHI is not improperly modified without detection until disposed of.

controls.title: Encryption (transmission)
controls.description: Implement a mechanism to encrypt ePHI whenever deemed appropriate.

controls.title: Business Associate Contracts — Standard
controls.description: The contract or other arrangement with a business associate must meet the applicable requirements of §164.314(a)(2).

controls.title: Business Associate Contracts — content requirements
controls.description: Business associate contracts must include provisions establishing permitted uses and disclosures, require the business associate to implement appropriate safeguards, report breaches, and ensure subcontractors comply with HIPAA.

controls.title: Other Arrangements — documentation of satisfactory assurances
controls.description: When a covered entity and business associate are both governmental entities or when other legal requirements exist, the covered entity may use a memorandum of understanding or other arrangement in lieu of a written contract.

controls.title: Business Associate Contracts — subcontractors
controls.description: A business associate that creates, receives, maintains, or transmits ePHI on behalf of another business associate must enter into a contract or arrangement that meets these requirements with each subcontractor.

controls.title: Requirements for Group Health Plans — Standard
controls.description: A group health plan that creates, receives, maintains, or transmits ePHI must ensure that its plan documents reasonably and appropriately safeguard ePHI and include provisions to support privacy and security compliance.

controls.title: Group Health Plan — implement administrative, physical, and technical safeguards
controls.description: The plan sponsor must implement administrative, physical, and technical safeguards that reasonably and appropriately protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of ePHI it creates, receives, maintains, or transmits.

controls.title: Policies and Procedures — Standard
controls.description: Implement reasonable and appropriate policies and procedures to comply with the standards, implementation specifications, or other requirements of this subpart.

controls.title: Documentation — Standard
controls.description: Maintain the policies and procedures implemented to comply with this subpart in written (which may be electronic) form.

controls.title: Documentation — Time Limit
controls.description: Retain required documentation for 6 years from the date of its creation or the date when it last was in effect, whichever is later.

controls.title: Documentation — Availability
controls.description: Make documentation available to those persons responsible for implementing the procedures to which the documentation pertains.

controls.title: Documentation — Updates
controls.description: Review documentation periodically and update as needed in response to environmental or operational changes affecting the security of ePHI.
